user_query:
Please set up a lightweight event-driven watcher that helps me catalogue the tone of my on-set memories. I drop plain-text story files into input/stories/inbox/, and I want the tool to automatically process each new file as it appears.

Requirements:
- Watch input/stories/inbox/ for new .txt files. On startup, immediately process any unprocessed files already in that folder, then continue watching until the process is stopped manually.
- Each story file starts with a header of exactly three lines:
  Title: <movie title>
  Year: <4-digit year>
  ---
  followed by the story text body.
- Use input/filmography.json to validate the header. If the (title, year) pair is not present there, mark validation=false and include a warning in the output; otherwise validation=true.
- Download the AFINN-111 English sentiment lexicon (file name: AFINN-111.txt) from the official AFINN repository hosted on GitHub (repository name: fnielsen/afinn). Cache the raw file at cache/lexicons/afinn/AFINN-111.txt and reuse it on subsequent runs if present. Do not hardcode lexicon content; actually download it. Record the lexicon source in outputs as "AFINN-111 (fnielsen/afinn)".
- Using that lexicon, compute for the story body:
  - word_count (token count, case-insensitive)
  - afinn_sentiment_score (sum of per-word AFINN scores)
  - top_positive_words and top_negative_words (up to 10 unique tokens each with positive/negative AFINN scores, ordered by descending frequency in the story; ties can be arbitrary). Only include words that appear in the lexicon.
- For each processed story file X.txt, write a summary JSON to output/stories/processed/X_summary.json with fields: input_file (relative path), title, year (number), word_count (number), afinn_sentiment_score (number), top_positive_words (array of strings), top_negative_words (array of strings), validation (boolean), warnings (array of strings, empty if none), time_processed (ISO 8601 string), lexicon_source (string), lexicon_cached_path (relative path).
- Maintain a processing registry at state/processed.jsonl. Each line is a JSON object with input_file, sha256 (of the story file contents), and time_processed. Skip reprocessing if a file with the same sha256 has already been recorded.
- Maintain an index CSV at output/stories/index.csv with columns: input_file,title,year,word_count,afinn_sentiment_score,time_processed. Append a new row for each newly processed file; update the row if the same input_file is reprocessed with a different sha256.

Inputs to rely on:
- input/filmography.json (my fictional filmography)
- Any .txt story files under input/stories/inbox/ (one sample is provided).

Deliverables (created by your automation):
- cache/lexicons/afinn/AFINN-111.txt (downloaded lexicon)
- state/processed.jsonl (line-delimited JSON registry)
- output/stories/processed/<input_basename>_summary.json for each processed story
- output/stories/index.csv (index of processed stories)

Please make sure the download step, directory inspection/watching, and structured data extraction from both the story files and the lexicon are all essential parts of the solution. I’m a nostalgic old pro—I just want to drop my memories in the inbox and see the summaries and index appear. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
